message:
feat(indexer): log budget-shrink decisions and message counts on lexical rebuild page prep

Add structured tracing and two new fields on `LexicalRebuildPagePrepWork`
so we can see, per ordered page in the lexical rebuild pipeline,
whether the configured page size or the pipeline byte budget was the
binding constraint. Previously the two paths joined before logging
and the budget-vs-configured decision was invisible in traces —
leaving us guessing why a rebuild was paginating 200 rows instead
of the configured 500 during memory-pressure windows.

Struct changes on LexicalRebuildPagePrepWork:
- `configured_page_size: i64` — echoes the configured page size at
  the point of prep (was previously lost after the caller computed
  the budget).
- `budget_generation: usize` — monotonically increasing counter the
  caller bumps whenever the pipeline budget is recomputed, so log
  consumers can correlate a "bounded_below_configured_page" event
  with the specific budget revision it ran against.

prepare_lexical_rebuild_page_work now emits a single
`tracing::info!` per page with:
    sequence, configured_page_size, page_conversation_limit,
    budget_generation, budget_shrink_decision,
    page_conversations, page_last_conversation_id, page_messages
where budget_shrink_decision is either:
    "bounded_below_configured_page" — the budget limit is below
      the configured page size, so the caller will process fewer
      conversations than configured; a human should investigate.
    "using_configured_page" — the budget allows the full
      configured page; nothing to worry about.

Also computes `page_message_count` by summing
`packet.message_count` across `prepared_packets` so the log line
reflects both conversation and message shapes of the current page.
The acquire-bytes path and flow-reservation assignment are unchanged
in behaviour; only the observability layer grew.
